case_title: SAMI YOUSSEF DDS VS. DENTAL BOARD OF CALFORNIA
cause_of_action: WRITS OF MANDATE OR PROH., CERTI., ETC./ADMIN. AGEN

docket_entries.description: DECLARATION IN SUPPORT OF MOTION FILED BY RESPONDENT DENTAL BOARD OF CALFORNIA
LAW AND MOTION 302, RESPONDENT DENTAL BOARD OF CALFORNIA'S MOTION TO CHANGE VENUE IS ORDERED OFF CALENDAR BY THE COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD E. KAHN; COURT REPORTER: LAVENA WARD CSR #7077
NOTICE OF HEARING FILED BY PETITIONER YOUSSEF DDS, SAMI
MEMORANDUM OF POINTS AND AUTHORITIES IN OPPOS TO MOTION TO CHANGE VENUE FILED BY PETITIONER YOUSSEF DDS, SAMI
ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR RESPONDENT DENTAL BOARD OF CALIFORNIA'S MOTION TO CHANGE VENUE HEARING SET FOR JUN-29-2012 AT 09:30 AM IN DEPT 302

LAW AND MOTION 302, THE MATTER WAS ARGUED. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. PETITIONER SAMI YOUSSEF DDS' MOTION TO STAY IS GRANTED. THERE IS GOOD CAUSE FOR THE REQUESTED RELIEF. THE CASE IS STAYED UNTIL SEPTEMBER 30, 2012 OR THE HEARING FOR THE WRIT, WHICHEVER IS EARLIER. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN, REPORTER: LAVENA WARD, CSR #7077

LAW AND MOTION 302, PETITIONER SAMI YOUSSEF'S MOTION FOR PEREMPTORY WRIT OF ADMINISTRATIVE MANDATE IS DENIED. PETITIONER WAS NOT DENIED A FAIR TRIAL. EACH SENTENCE OF THE CHARGE IS NOT A SEPARATE ELEMENT THAT MUST BE PROVEN. THERE IS NO INDICATION IN THE RECORD THAT RESPONDENT IMPROPERLY SHIFTED THE BURDEN TO PETITIONER. THE ADMINISTRATIVE LAW JUDGE DID NOT COMMIT A PREJUDICIAL ABUSE OF DISCRETION. THE ADMINISTRATIVE LAW JUDGE FOUND THAT PETITIONER MISREPRESENTED AB'S PORTION OF THE COST OF HER DENTAL CARE "HIMSELF, OR THROUGH HIS STAFF'" (AR00035). THERE WAS SUFFICIENT EVIDENCE TO SHOW THAT PETITIONER EITHER MADE THE REPRESENTATIONS HIMSELF OR HAD KNOWLEDGE OF AND RATIFIED THE REPRESENTATIONS REGARDING PAYMENT. RESPONDENT DID NOT NEED TO ESTABLISH FRAUD IN ORDER TO PREVAIL ON ITS CHARGE. RESPONDENT WAS NOT REQUIRED TO PROVIDE EXPERT TESTIMONY TO SUPPORT THE CHARGE. THE DECISION IS SUPPORTED BY THE FINDINGS, AND THE FINDINGS ARE SUPPORTED BY THE EVIDENCE. UPON INDEPENDENT REVIEW, THE COURT AGREES WITH THE ADMINISTRATIVE LAW JUDGE'S FINDING THAT PETITIONER'S TESTIMONY ABOUT THE AMOUNT BILLED TO AB AND STATEMENTS MADE TO AB REGARDING THIS AMOUNT WAS CONFUSING AND INCONSISTENT. RESPONDENT'S OBJECTIONS ARE OVERRULED. THE COURT ORDERS COUNSEL FOR RESPONDENT TO SUBMIT PROPOSED STATEMENT OF DECISION. JUDGE: MARLA MILLER, CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, REPORTER: DENISE DOUCETTE, #5963.
MINI MINUTES FOR DEC-12-2012 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 302

calendar.matters:
Notice Of Motion For Peremptory Writ Of Administritve Mandate
Off Calendar Dec-12-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: William E. Manning, Esq., 925.938.1430, for petitioner Sammi Youssef.
Brian Turner, Esq., 916.445.0803, for respondent Dental Board of California.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling: PETITIONER SAMI YOUSSEF'S Motion For Peremptory Writ Of Administrative Mandate is denied. Petitioner was not denied a fair trial. Each sentence of the charge is not a separate element that must be proven. There is no indication in the record that Respondent improperly shifted the burden to Petitioner. The administrative law judge did not commit a prejudicial abuse of discretion. The administrative law judge found that Petitioner misrepresented AB's portion of the cost of her dental care "himself, or through his staff'" (AR00035). There was sufficient evidence to show that Petitioner either made the representations himself or had knowledge of and ratified the representations regarding payment. Respondent did not need to establish fraud in order to prevail on its charge. Respondent was not required to provide expert testimony to support the charge. The Decision is supported by the findings, and the findings are supported by the evidence. Upon independent review, the Court agrees with the administrative law judge's finding that Petitioner's testimony about the amount billed to AB and statements made to AB regarding this amount was confusing and inconsistent. Respondent's objections are overruled. The Court orders counsel for respondent to submit proposed statement of decision. Judge: Marla Miller, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Denise Doucette, #5963.

Notice Of Motion For Peremptory Writ Of Administritve Mandate
Off Calendar Nov-02-2012 Continued To Dec-04-12 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:

NO APPEARANCES. THE COURT ADOPTS THE TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS:

PETITIONER SAMI YOUSSEF'S Motion For Peremptory Writ Of Administritve Mandate is continued to December 4, 2012, to be heard on the law and motion calendar at 9:30 a.m. The motion was mis-calendared on the discovery calendar and the Court needs a courtesy copy of the administrative record. Petitioner shall lodge a copy of the administrative record by November 12, 2012, with a letter indicating the new hearing date. Nils Rosenquest, Judge Pro-Tem, Jacqueline Alameda, Clerk, Not Reported. =302/JPT

Respondent Dental Board Of California'S Motion To Change Venue
Off Calendar Jun-29-2012 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Brian Turner, Esq., Deputy Attorney General, Tel. 916.445.0603, appearing for respondent and moving party;
William E. Manning, Esq., of Sellar Hazard Manning Ficenec & Lucia, Tel. 925.938.1430, appearing for petitioner and opposing party.

There being no opposition, the court adopted its tentative ruling. RESPONDENT DENTAL BOARD OF CALIFORNIA'S MOTION TO CHANGE VENUE is Denied. The petition was properly filed in San Francisco County pursuant to Code of Civil Procedure section 401(1). Order signed in open Court. Judge: Harold Kahn; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer CSR # 5976

Petitioner Sami Youssef Dds' Motion To Stay
Off Calendar Jun-29-2012 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Brian Turner, Esq., Deputy Attorney General, Tel. 916.445.0603, appearing for respondent and moving party;
William E. Manning, Esq., of Sellar Hazard Manning Ficenec & Lucia, Tel. 925.938.1430, appearing for petitioner and opposing party.

The matter was argued. The court adopted its tentative ruling. PETITIONER SAMI YOUSSEF DDS' MOTION TO STAY is Granted. There is good cause for the requested relief. The case is stayed until September 30, 2012 or the hearing for the writ, whichever is earlier. Order signed in open Court. Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR #7077
